So now that you have a foundation of the straight line system from the ebook, I want to take you inside a live event. So you actually see me going over this stuff live right on video. And let me just set this clip up for you here. It really starts, and if you've seen the movie you probably know some of this, where on my first day on Wall Street where I became a stockbroker it was actually Black Monday, where the market went down 508 points in a single day. Just like that, after watching all these other brokers get rich and I was their slave just you know smiling and dialing right and passing the phone through another broker. My turn came and bam the firm was out of business. That night when I came home and I you know walked through that door and you know my wife had no idea if there was a wife number one back then and no idea that the market had even crashed. It wasn't much of a news bug. And that was really when I came home I collapsed in her arms and I took that first punch that we all take as entrepreneurs and also salespeople. I took that punch and I was literally paralyzed with fear and uncertainty. All my hopes and dreams had been dashed with the market crashing and I was out of a job. At first I was going to leave Wall Street altogether and I started looking through the help wanted section of the paper and I stumbled upon an ad for a brokerage firm in Long Island. And that, and you remember I'm sure from the movie it's a classic scene when I walk into this firm and these guys are sitting around they're all disheveled, you know someone going to the bathroom the toilet flushes right and I walk in there and they're selling penny stocks. And again I didn't know what a penny stock was at the time and when I heard these guys selling on the phone they were just lying, they were just terrible salespeople. They most of them couldn't even sell the weight of a paper bag basically right they were just awful salespeople. And I sat down and I realized the commissions were huge and they handed me a list of seven stocks so I picked one which I think it was the dog with the least fleas it was called Oncliffe National and I started writing myself a script. And again this is pretty straight line just my own ideas of what I should be saying on the phone to close the deal. And I heard a lot of stuff those seven or eight months when I was training on Wall Street right. Now it was my chance to shine on the telephone. You know I wrote the script and when it was done it was really really good and I picked up the phone the first person I called the words just started coming out perfectly. I had this knack for selling on the telephone. And as I and this is a classic scene again from the movie where they're watching me and they you know they can't believe what they're hearing because I sound so good on the phone and I did and I ended up closing the guy for you know four or five thousand dollars of this Oncliffe National. And when I'm done the guy's like you know how'd you do that what were you doing and from that point forward I developed this little sort of cult following of these you know young guys that would listen to me and I was training them it was pretty straight line we're selling penny stocks to average moms and pops pretty easy sales far as you know my book right. And then ultimately I opened up my own firm and then when I made that transition I spoke about before and started selling to the richest one percent of Americans those five dollar stocks that was when the system broke down. This is before the straight line very often with systems you don't really know how good they are until you stress it at the highest level and that's when the cracks the fishers start to appear. And when I went to this sort of high level selling when you know you're calling rich people was sophisticated you know they you know their owners people of power and these young guys are trying to call them with some sort of you know very disorganized system I had back then before the straight line it broke down. And that was really what forced me to invent this unbelievably effective way of taking any human being and show them how they can become a world class closer by essentially taking a step back before they start to sell and realizing some very fundamental things about any situation of influence any negotiation anything at all having to do with selling or you try to get someone to do something or believe something or buy into an idea that you have and it starts with this understanding that there are certain things that need to line up in every sale and those things are always the same and by putting them in a straight line a linear fashion bam what happens is truly staggering in terms of you being able to close the deal. voice the rhythm the tones to imply hidden meanings to the words that you say what do you think happens when something's italicized in writing and you stress it or there's a question mark where it shouldn't be in a sales letter and something is bold and something is in light type it's tonality on paper I'm an author and I it took me I found it very difficult to become an author writing did not tonality influencing becomes really really easy because the words the language patterns are there I can hang I'll hand them to you on a silver platter the language patterns to influence are there some of them border on hypnotic language patterns some of them are very basic and just cut to logical ways the brain works some cut to emotional reasons why people take action but the language patterns are easy what people don't pay enough attention to is what really influences people which is tonality and secondly your body language the second and here's the irony of body language when it's in person people start sizing up in one twenty fourth of a second one they just study what is meant to a brain machine they can actually see what's happening as you see a new person and they saw it boom one twenty fourth of a second the spot up and down huge spike and you're down the brain calculates that fast in body language so when I teach people how to become effective influencers and closers it's not about become some hard charging you know cliched sales person sometimes that's called for in some industries but more often it's not about that more often it's about being mellow and being persuasive by using tonalities that are pleasing to the ear and that inserts subliminal messages as you're talking and here's what those messages are number one a that you care I care this is the number one most important thing that has to be established on a subconscious not a conscious tell someone I really care about you they'll say what a freaking lion sack of shit salesman this is I care on they won't even know why the people will feel that wow this guy really cares this girl she really cares about me so number one is the established that you care number two that you're just like them people associate people make decisions want to do business with people who are like them not who are opposite of them when you go out and join a club a country club or any organization you don't say wow this is a really great place there's not a soul there who's like me no you go and say wow everyone else cool place they have shared my interest they're just like me so you join you associate when you're trying to influence somebody okay the perception has to be I'm just like you now the mistake most sales people make okay and what I'm getting at here is something called starts with R and want to guess it rapport right which is a where we all know well which is the most misunderstood word in the English language in my book it's ridiculous what people think constitutes rapport is often I'm like fuck well they want me to think we three hours to you to take my three hours I had these guys captain for years see what happened was so I started teaching these guys a straight line system and I'll show you visually what the straight line is I called it the straight line because out of sheer frustration like every every morning I give a meeting for about an hour and every afternoon I give another meeting for about an hour and a half every day for five years as the firm started with 12 people and grew to thousands and imagine a room about twice the size of this packed from front to back with desks okay people banging away on the phone pacing around what was absolutely insane you walk me like holy shit is this an alternative universe that's what happened ultimately but in the beginning it was 12 guys okay and trying to explain to my don't I was like don't you fucking get to I was like I was killing me inside they were against it every sale is the fucking same they're like why like every sale is the same every encounter I feel like we're even negotiation every sale is the same I'm like watch it's a fucking straight line here's your open here and I was like that's how fresh I said here's the open here's the clothes right now there's gonna be every sale they'll try to go off the line right go here and here below and above right these are the boundaries you will not allow the encounter to go outside the boundaries and that is called what controlling the sale at the essence of effective communication at the essence of influence in your business right now once you learn to control the linguistic encounter you will be shocked at what will happen so I taught them number one to control the sale here's the perfect sale or they agree with everything you say from the first word you order they're on board with you they're saying yes to every question you ask perfect sale how often does that happen not too often right so what happens is is the customer has a tendency the prospect to go off the line they want to go off the line you want to keep them on the line what you have here between here and here is your healthy boundaries of where the sale can go as long as the sale stays within these boundaries you're in control if it goes outside the boundaries you're out of control in the boundaries there's two things that you're doing number one you're establishing instant massive rapport through tonality and body language and number two using that rapport to gather intelligence to find out what the prospect truly needs what his true desires are because here's the deal remember I said those three things what are they sharpest attack or sharpest attack enthusiastic and what else expert expert you feel those three things right but why what is what is the higher purposes what it really means is this what this all rolls up to one thing is that you are a person worth listening to and taking it one step higher than that because you're someone who could help them achieve their goals you could help them get what they want that's why these three things are so important because they chunk up to a higher purpose which is that you're a guy that could help them or a girl who could help them get what they want in life if they don't think you could help them they're not going to want to talk to you.
